novel_features.name: Instructor scorecard
novel_features.description: Rank instructors by class fill rate, no-show rate, and check-in rate across your synced schedule.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining classes and reservations and aggregating per instructor_name; the Sutra API returns no per-instructor aggregates.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when you need to compare teacher performance to decide who to coach or feature, not raw class rows.

novel_features.name: No-show rate
novel_features.description: Surface no-show rates grouped by instructor, class, or client from synced reservations.
novel_features.rationale: Aggregates reservations with status NO_SHOW over booked; the platform buries this inside a fixed Attendance report.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when you need to quantify and pivot no-shows, e.g. which instructor or client drives the most absences.

novel_features.name: Capacity utilization
novel_features.description: Compute fill ratio (booked vs capacity) per class, instructor, time-slot, or location over a date window.
novel_features.rationale: Segmenting total_booked/max_capacity across the schedule is local-only; no single endpoint aggregates it.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to find under-filled slots and over-subscribed classes before schedule planning.

novel_features.name: Expiring / low-balance watchlist
novel_features.description: List active memberships and class-packs expiring within a window or running low on credits, with client contact info.
novel_features.rationale: Filters purchases by end_date and remaining_uses and joins client contact details; ranking plus contact join is local-only.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use for deterministic renewal outreach: who needs to re-up this week, with their email and phone.

novel_features.name: Churn / at-risk clients
novel_features.description: Flag non-removed clients with no recent check-in and/or an expired plan using a mechanical recency threshold.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining clients, reservations, and purchases to detect behavioral lapse; impossible from one call.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to build a win-back list of members drifting away, distinct from hard date-based expiry.

novel_features.name: Revenue with prior-period comparison
novel_features.description: Sum purchase revenue by membership type for a window and show the delta versus the prior equal window.
novel_features.rationale: Prior-period comparison is the explicit feature operators ask for and the canned vendor reports omit; computed from local purchases.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use for the Monday revenue review: is this week up or down versus last, by membership type.

novel_features.name: Referral funnel conversion
novel_features.description: Trace referrals to whether the referred client signed up, purchased, and attended, and rank top referrers.
novel_features.rationale: Walks referrals to clients to purchases to reservations — a three-table funnel no Sutra endpoint exposes.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to measure whether your referral program actually converts and who your best referrers are.

novel_features.name: Client lifetime value
novel_features.description: Rank clients by total purchase spend with tenure since signup.
novel_features.rationale: Per-client lifetime totals require summing purchases per client and joining tenure; multi-table aggregation only.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use to identify your highest-value members for VIP outreach or to understand revenue concentration.

novel_features_built.name: Instructor scorecard
novel_features_built.description: Rank instructors by class fill rate, no-show rate, and check-in rate across your synced schedule.

narrative.headline: Every Sutra (Arketa) Partner API resource plus a local mirror and the studio analytics the dashboard buries.
narrative.value_prop: The Sutra/Arketa Partner API is pure CRUD with zero reporting endpoints, so studio operators are stuck with canned vendor reports they cannot customize and a client list the vendor is cagey about exporting. This CLI syncs your studio's full dataset (locations, classes, clients, purchases, referrals, reservations) into a local SQLite database you own, then answers the questions the dashboard hides: instructor scorecards, no-show rates, capacity utilization, expiring memberships, churn risk, referral conversion, client LTV, and revenue with prior-period comparison. The daily front-desk loop (book, cancel, check-in) is in here too, all offline-queryable and agent-native.
narrative.auth_narrative: Authentication uses a partner API key plus your partner ID. Set SUTRA_API_KEY to the key Arketa issued you (sent as the X-API-Key header) and SUTRA_PARTNER_ID to your partner identifier, which scopes every request. Run 'sutra-fitness-pp-cli doctor' to confirm both are set and the API is reachable before syncing.

quickstart.comment: Confirm SUTRA_API_KEY and SUTRA_PARTNER_ID are configured before any live call.
Pull your studio's full dataset into a local SQLite mirror you own (run this first).
See fill rate per instructor across the synced schedule.
List memberships and class-packs expiring in the next week for renewal outreach.
Rank instructors by fill, no-show, and check-in rate.

troubleshoots.fix: Set SUTRA_API_KEY to your Arketa partner key; it is sent as the X-API-Key header. Verify with 'sutra-fitness-pp-cli doctor'.

troubleshoots.symptom: 403 Forbidden / access denied to partner data
troubleshoots.fix: Set SUTRA_PARTNER_ID to your own partner identifier; the key only grants access to its own partner's data.

troubleshoots.symptom: Analytics commands return empty or 'no local mirror'
troubleshoots.fix: Run 'sutra-fitness-pp-cli sync --resources classes,clients,purchases,reservations' first; analytics read the local store.
Re-run 'sutra-fitness-pp-cli sync --since 24h' to pull recently updated records via the updated_at cursor.

troubleshoots.symptom: 429 / rate limited during a large sync
troubleshoots.fix: The API caps at ~25 req/sec; re-run sync (it resumes from the cursor) or narrow with --resources and --since.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an operator or agent needs to answer studio-operations questions the Arketa dashboard cannot: no-show rates by instructor, capacity utilization across the schedule, which memberships expire this week, churn risk, referral conversion, client lifetime value, or revenue with prior-period comparison. It is also the fastest path for the front-desk loop of listing rosters, booking, canceling, and checking in reservations. Sync first, then query the local mirror offline.
anti_triggers: Charging cards, processing payments, or issuing arbitrary refunds — the API exposes purchases read-only and only supports reservation cancel/refund flags, not money movement.
Creating or editing classes, schedules, rooms, or locations — these are read-only in the Partner API.
Creating or editing client profiles — clients are read-only; only reservations can be created, canceled, or checked in.
Real-time live dashboards or streaming monitors — this is a sync-then-query tool, not a live feed.

recipes.title: Monday revenue review
recipes.explanation: Totals revenue by membership type for the current window and shows the delta versus the prior equal window.

recipes.title: Renewal outreach list
recipes.explanation: Exports a CSV of members expiring this week or low on credits, with contact info, ready for an email or SMS campaign.

recipes.title: Agent-friendly instructor ranking
recipes.explanation: Returns a narrowed, machine-readable instructor ranking so an agent can reason over fill and no-show rates without parsing the full nested payload.

recipes.title: At-risk client sweep
recipes.explanation: Lists members with no check-in in 30 days or an expired plan as a JSON win-back list.
